summary: High‑energy Fresno show with epic Lovelight and Pigpen organ
blurb: The second half erupts into a 21‑minute Turn On Your Lovelight featuring guest Ronnie Hawkins, while Pigpen’s organ shines on Sitting On Top Of The World. Jerry Garcia delivers powerful solos throughout and the rhythm section drives the band forward. Clear soundboard capture makes it an essential late‑60s listening experience.
review: On June 13, 1969 at the Fresno Convention Center the Dead turned up the voltage. After a tight first set the band broke loose on the second half, launching into a monster Turn On Your Lovelight that stretches past twenty minutes and showcases guest vocalist Ronnie Hawkins adding a blues‑rock edge. Pigpen’s organ work on Sitting On Top Of The World is a standout moment, giving the song an extra lift while Bob Weir adds vocal flair. Jerry Garcia tears through his solos, especially during the Saint Stephen → The Eleven → Lovelight sequence, and Phil Lesh “valiantly rips” on bass, holding the jam together. Mickey Hart and Bill Kreutzmann keep the groove dancing behind the kit, and Tom Constanten’s keys sit cleanly in the mix. The soundboard source is crisp and captures every nuance, making this recording a clear window into one of the Dead’s high‑energy late‑summer outings.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All available analysis is positive, praising the high‑energy performance, epic Lovelight jam, and clear soundboard capture. Although only a single recording was reviewed, the language is consistently enthusiastic (“high‑energy”, “epic 21‑minute Lovelight”, “clear and top‑notch”), justifying a solid positive adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Clear soundboard with good overall fidelity, captures the 21‑minute Lovelight and Pigpen’s organ work without major dropouts.

key_highlights: Epic 21‑minute Turn On Your Lovelight featuring guest Ronnie Hawkins.
Pigpen’s organ shines on Sitting On Top Of The World.
Strong transitions and jam flow through Saint Stephen → The Eleven → Lovelight.
Crisp soundboard recording that preserves the band’s high‑energy performance.
song_highlights: Sitting On Top Of The World

band_performance.Jerry: Tore it up on guitar, leading the Lovelight jam and delivering powerful solos; a brief “wrong dose” moment noted but overall a highlight.
band_performance.Phil: Valiantly ripping during the Saint Stephen > Eleven > Lovelight sequence, providing solid bass foundation throughout.
band_performance.Bob: Exceptional vocal contributions and rap sections; interacts well with Pigpen’s organ, adding texture to the performance.
band_performance.Mickey: Dancing behind the kit, supplying a propulsive rhythmic drive that fuels the jams.
band_performance.Billy: Dancing alongside Mickey, delivering tight drum work that underpins the high‑energy set.

must_listen_sequences: Sitting On Top Of The World
